cards.front: Write the fundamental equation defining overall Closed-Loop Gain (A_f) using A_d and β.
What does the equation A_f = A_d / (1 + A_d * β) approximate to when A_d is extremely large?
Why is it vital that A_f ≈ 1 / β?
cards.back: It proves the closed-loop gain depends completely on the stable external physical resistors (β) and is totally immune to internal temperature/manufacturing fluctuations of the op-amp.
For a standard op-amp, the multiplication of its closed-loop Gain by its maximum Bandwidth is a perfectly constant number (e.g., 1 MHz for an LM741).

cards.front: If an op-amp has a GBP of 10 MHz, what is its maximum bandwidth if configured as a Unity Gain Buffer (Gain = 1)?
Why does the Open-Loop gain of an op-amp drop so aggressively after 10 Hz?
cards.back: Because manufacturers intentionally place a microscopic compensation capacitor inside the silicon to act as a low-pass filter, killing high-frequency gain to prevent the op-amp from oscillating.

cards.front: How do you physically trade gain for bandwidth in an op-amp circuit?
cards.back: By increasing the negative feedback fraction (β). The tighter you clamp down the massive open-loop gain to a much smaller closed-loop gain (like 10), the further out the bandwidth extends before hitting the curve.

cards.front: What does a roll-off of -20 dB/decade mean in voltage terms?
cards.back: It means every time the frequency multiplies by 10 (one decade), the voltage gain divides exactly by 10.

cards.front: What is the Unity Gain Frequency (f_T)?
cards.back: The incredibly high frequency where the open-loop gain curve finally crosses the 0 dB line (a voltage gain of exactly 1). It is numerically equal to the GBP.

cards.front: If you need both massive gain AND massive bandwidth that exceeds your op-amp's GBP, what is a circuit topology solution?
cards.back: Cascading (daisy-chaining). Use multiple op-amp stages set to very low individual gains (which preserves their high bandwidths). Their low gains will multiply together to create massive overall gain.
